sentence: If I travel during the low season, I save money.
translation: Si viajo en temporada baja, ahorro dinero.
explanation: Zero Conditional: If + present simple, present simple. Used for general truths or facts.

sentence: If you book flights early, you get better prices.
translation: Si reservas vuelos temprano, obtienes mejores precios.
explanation: Zero Conditional: If + present simple, present simple. Used for general truths or facts.

sentence: If people travel light, they move more easily.
translation: Si las personas viajan ligero, se mueven más fácilmente.
explanation: Zero Conditional: If + present simple, present simple. Used for general truths or facts.

sentence: If it rains during the trip, we stay inside.
translation: Si llueve durante el viaje, nos quedamos adentro.
explanation: Zero Conditional: If + present simple, present simple. Used for general truths or facts.

sentence: If you visit new places, you learn new cultures.
translation: Si visitas nuevos lugares, aprendes nuevas culturas.
explanation: Zero Conditional: If + present simple, present simple. Used for general truths or facts.

sentence: If I save enough money, I will travel to Japan next year.
translation: Si ahorro suficiente dinero, viajaré a Japón el próximo año.
explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb. Used for real and possible future situations.

sentence: If we book the tickets today, we will get a discount.
translation: Si reservamos los boletos hoy, obtendremos un descuento.
explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb. Used for real and possible future situations.

sentence: If she finishes her project, she will join us on the trip.
translation: Si ella termina su proyecto, se unirá a nosotros en el viaje.
explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb. Used for real and possible future situations.

sentence: If the weather is good, we will go hiking tomorrow.
translation: Si el clima es bueno, iremos a hacer senderismo mañana.
explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb. Used for real and possible future situations.

sentence: If I find cheap flights, I will travel more often.
translation: Si encuentro vuelos baratos, viajaré más a menudo.
explanation: First Conditional: If + present simple, will + base verb. Used for real and possible future situations.

sentence: If I had more money, I would travel around the world.
translation: Si tuviera más dinero, viajaría por todo el mundo.
explanation: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. Used for hypothetical or unlikely situations in the present/future.

sentence: If we had more time, we would visit more countries.
translation: Si tuviéramos más tiempo, visitaríamos más países.
explanation: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. Used for hypothetical or unlikely situations in the present/future.

sentence: If she knew how to drive, she would rent a car.
translation: Si ella supiera conducir, alquilaría un carro.
explanation: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. Used for hypothetical or unlikely situations in the present/future.

sentence: If I were you, I would plan the trip earlier.
translation: Si yo fuera tú, planearía el viaje antes.
explanation: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. Used for hypothetical or unlikely situations in the present/future.

sentence: If they lived closer, they would travel with us.
translation: Si vivieran más cerca, viajarían con nosotros.
explanation: Second Conditional: If + past simple, would + base verb. Used for hypothetical or unlikely situations in the present/future.

sentence: If I had booked earlier, I would have paid less for the flight.
translation: Si hubiera reservado antes, habría pagado menos por el vuelo.
explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Used for unreal situations in the past.

sentence: If we had checked the weather, we would have avoided the storm.
translation: Si hubiéramos revisado el clima, habríamos evitado la tormenta.
explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Used for unreal situations in the past.

sentence: If she had taken a map, she would have found the place easily.
translation: Si hubiera llevado un mapa, habría encontrado el lugar fácilmente.
explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Used for unreal situations in the past.

sentence: If they had left earlier, they would have arrived on time.
translation: Si hubieran salido antes, habrían llegado a tiempo.
explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Used for unreal situations in the past.

sentence: If I had known about the delay, I would have stayed at the hotel.
translation: Si hubiera sabido sobre el retraso, me habría quedado en el hotel.
explanation: Third Conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle. Used for unreal situations in the past.

sentence: If I had traveled more when I was younger, I would be more experienced now.
translation: Si hubiera viajado más cuando era joven, ahora tendría más experiencia.
explanation: Mixed Conditional: Combination of second and third conditionals. Used to connect past conditions with present results.

sentence: If she had studied abroad, she would have more opportunities today.
translation: Si hubiera estudiado en el extranjero, tendría más oportunidades hoy.
explanation: Mixed Conditional: Combination of second and third conditionals. Used to connect past conditions with present results.

sentence: If we were more organized, we would have planned a better trip.
translation: Si fuéramos más organizados, habríamos planeado un mejor viaje.
explanation: Mixed Conditional: Combination of second and third conditionals. Used to connect present conditions with past results.

sentence: If I were more adventurous, I would have tried local food.
translation: Si fuera más aventurero, habría probado comida local.
explanation: Mixed Conditional: Combination of second and third conditionals. Used to connect present conditions with past results.

sentence: If they had saved more money, they would be traveling now.
translation: Si hubieran ahorrado más dinero, ahora estarían viajando.
explanation: Mixed Conditional: Combination of second and third conditionals. Used to connect past conditions with present results.
